title: Reverse Words in a String

content.problemStatement:
You are given a string sss consisting of English letters, digits, and spaces `' '`.

Your task is to **reverse the order of the words** in the string.

A word is defined as a sequence of non-space characters.

The words in sss are separated by one or more spaces.

You must:



Return the words in **reverse order**, separated by a **single space**.




Remove any **leading**, **trailing**, or **multiple spaces** between words.



Function DeclarationFunction NamereverseWordsreverseWordsreverseWords – This function reverses the order of words in a given string while removing extra spaces.

Parameters
sss : A reference to a string consisting of English letters (uppercase and lowercase), digits, and spaces.
Return Value
Returns a string where:

The order of words is reversed.
Words are separated by a single space.
Leading, trailing, and multiple spaces are removed.


Constraints
1≤T≤1001 \leq T \leq 1001≤T≤100
1≤∣s∣≤1041 \leq |s| \leq 10^41≤∣s∣≤104
The string contains English letters (uppercase and lowercase), digits, and spaces.
There is at least one word in the string.

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each of the next TTT lines contains a string sss.


Output Format

For each test case, print a single line containing the string with words in reverse order, separated by a single space.
constraints: s contains only letters (a‑z, A‑Z), digits (0‑9) and space characters
There is at least one word in each s
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string s consisting of English letters, digits and spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the words of s in reverse order, separated by exactly one space. All leading, trailing and multiple intermediate spaces must be removed.

sampleTestCases.input:
4
codechef is awesome
  java   and   python  
123 test case
   learn   data   structures
sampleTestCases.output:
awesome is codechef
python and java
case test 123
structures data learn
sampleTestCases.explanation: Each line is split into words, the order of the words is reversed and they are printed with a single space.
First test case: words are a, b, c → reversed c b a. Second test case contains only one word, so it stays unchanged.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.input:
1
word1 word2 word3 word4 word5 word6 word7 word8 word9 word10 word11 word12 word13 word14 word15
hidden.expectedOutput: word15 word14 word13 word12 word11 word10 word9 word8 word7 word6 word5 word4 word3 word2 word1

hidden.label: uniform case 2
hidden.input:
1
x y x y x y
hidden.expectedOutput: y x y x y x

hidden.label: tricky case
hidden.input:
1
a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a
hidden.expectedOutput: a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a

hidden.label: tricky case 2
hidden.input:
1
longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword
hidden.expectedOutput: longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword longwordlongwordlongword

hidden.label: stress case
hidden.input:
3
first test
second   test case
   third
hidden.expectedOutput:
test first
case test second
third

hidden.label: stress case 3
hidden.input:
4
one
two three
four five six
seven eight nine ten
hidden.expectedOutput:
one
three two
six five four
ten nine eight seven

solution.approach: For each test case we split the input line into words ignoring any number of spaces. This can be done by trimming the line and then using a regular expression that matches one or more whitespace characters ("\\s+"). The resulting array contains the words in original order. We then output the words in reverse order, joining them with a single space. This removes leading, trailing and duplicate spaces automatically. The algorithm runs in linear time with respect to the length of the string and uses only O(number of words) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(|s|) per test case, O(total input size) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(W) where W is the number of words in the current test case (used for the split array).
